item.text:
The leak is in the sliding-window bookkeeping: if `allow()` only appends `now` and checks `len(self.calls)`, timestamps older than `window` stay in `self.calls` forever. Over time, every request leaves one float behind, so memory grows with total historical calls instead of active in-window calls.

The minimal fix is to prune expired timestamps at the start of `allow()` before checking the limit:

```python
def allow(self) -> bool:
    now = time.time()
    self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
    if len(self.calls) < self.limit:
        self.calls.append(now)
        return True
    return False
```

That line is already present in [demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-nohook/demo/rate_limiter.py:15), and the regression test in [tests/test_rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-nohook/tests/test_rate_limiter.py:8) passes:

`PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest tests.test_rate_limiter` → OK.
